title: Chromolaena austera
intro: Chromolaena odorata, also known as Chromolaena austera, is a tropical plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in many parts of the world. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in treating various ailments including fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Chromolaena austera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chromolaena austera is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as tinctures and ointments, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine.
taxonomy: Chromolaena austera is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Senecioneae and the genus Chromolaena. The genus Chromolaena is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the "tree marigolds" or "tickseeds". This species is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, but has been naturalized in many other parts of the world. Chromolaena austera is often considered an invasive species due to its ability to outcompete native plants for resources.
